trails.description: This track starts at Tarawera Outlet and finishes at Humphries Bay. This area was extensively altered by the 1886 Tarawera eruption and the landscape is being slowly regenerated by native plant species.

Tikitapu Nature Walk
trails.description: This short walk through beautiful forest has information panels about plant and birds along the way - it's a great option for children.

trails.name: Central Waiau hut to Te Totara int. RT
Te Tihi o Tawa MTB track
Aratiatia Rapids tracks
trails.description: Several times a day the gates at the Aratiatia dam are opened to release the full flow of the river - a spectacular sight from the lookout points. Stay safe and keep clear of the rapids at all times.

trails.name: Arohaki Lagoon Track
trails.description: Arohaki Lagoon is one of the must see sites in the Whirinaki.

trails.name: Blue Lake Track
trails.description: Enjoy native bush and quiet beaches. The track provides good opportunities to look out over the Blue Lake (Tikitapu) and Green Lake (Rotokakahi).

trails.name: Lake Ōkataina walking tracks
trails.description: Walk one of the numerous short walks at Lake Ōkataina Scenic Reserve or link them together for a longer walk.

trails.name: H Tree Track
trails.description: The H Tree Track in Whirinaki Te Pua-a-Tāne Conservation Park is an easy loop track  through grand podocarp forest. The forest is home to a number of native birds, including kaka.

trails.name: Hinehopu/Hongi's Track
trails.description: This track highlights the area's incredible native forest and cultural importance.  Beautiful beach at Korokitewao Bay (Lake Rotoiti) for swimming and picnicking.

trails.name: Huka Falls tracks
trails.description: Take a scenic walk along the banks of the Waikato River, or simply explore the many lookout points at Huka Falls, one of the most visited natural attractions in New Zealand - these short, easy walks are great for kids.

trails.name: Isthmus Track
trails.description: This track is dominated by the majestic Mount Tarawera.

trails.name: Eastern Ōkataina Walkway
trails.description: Enjoy splendid lake views through native bush with the option to branch off the track and head down to Lake Ōkataina at several bays.

trails.name: Mokaihaha Track
trails.description: Large podocarps and a chance to hear kaka and other native birdlife while walking along the Mokaihaha Track which takes you past Lake Rotohokahoka.

trails.name: Mt Ngongotaha Jubilee Track
trails.description: The only walking track up to the summit of Mt Ngongotaha. Passes through native forest with views of one of the largest rata trees in the Bay of Plenty.

trails.name: Mt Ngongotaha Nature Loop Track
trails.description: This short walk is close to Rotorua and showcases the beautiful flora and fauna of Mt Ngongotaha. It is ideal for families or school groups.

trails.name: Okere Falls Track
trails.description: With viewing platforms that provide great views of magnificent  waterfalls and white water rafters making their way down the Kaituna River, as well as geocaches and glow worms at night, there is plenty to keep kids entertained on this short, easy walk.

trails.name: Opepe walks
trails.description: Perfect for families and just a short drive from Taupō on SH5, explore native bush and discover local history in this scenic and historic reserve.

trails.name: Rotary Ride and Waikato River Track
trails.description: These two tracks along the banks of the Waikato River allow for stunning views of the river, and access to Huka Falls and the network of mountain bike trails in the area.

trails.name: Tarawera Falls Track
trails.description: The track runs between the Waterfall road carpark to the spectacular falls, where water surges out of fissures in a high cliff face. Limited access times and permits required.

trails.name: Tarawera Outlet to Falls Track
trails.description: See the Tarawera River disappearing underground at various sites before it re-emerges through the spectacular Tarawera Falls through narrow fissures in a fractured rhyolite lava cliff.

trails.name: Tarawera Outlet to Humphries Bay
trails.description: This area was extensively altered by the 1886 Tarawera eruption and the landscape is being slowly regenerated by native plant species.

trails.name: Tarawera Trail
trails.description: Meander your way through bush from Te Wairoa to Hot Water Beach Campsite. Stay overnight or book a water taxi to The Landing.

trails.name: Tuhoto Ariki Trail
trails.description: Specifically designed dual use trail through native bush with great downhills and some decent climbs for the mountain bikers. Starts from the highest point in Whakarewarewa Forest.

trails.name: Waiatiu Falls Track
trails.description: A 1 hour 30 minutes return walk, the Waiatiu Falls Track in Whirinaki Te Pua-a-Tāne Conservation Park leads through podocarp forest before reaching the picturesque falls.

trails.name: Western Ōkataina Walkway
trails.description: Western Ōkataina Walkway is open for both walking and mountain biking.

trails.name: Whirinaki Waterfall Loop Track
trails.description: On the Whirinaki Waterfall Loop Track you'll follow the Whirinaki River as it flows through podocarp forest.

trails.name: Sanctuary Track
trails.description: The Sanctuary Track is an easy loop track that goes through remnant podocarp forest. You can also walk it at night as the Whirinaki Night Life Walk.

trails.name: Mangamate Loop Track
trails.description: The loop covers a range of track types including multiple river crossing. This makes it an ideal introduction to backcountry tramping.

trails.name: Whirinaki Recreation Camp tracks
trails.description: Walk through native forest, along the Whirinaki River or uphill to great views, on these short walks from the Whirinaki Recreation Camp.

trails.name: Lake Ōkāreka Loop [Te Ara O Tūkonohi]
trails.description:
The Lake Okareka Loop Track is a diverse 9 km walk, ranging from easy to moderate, with some sections wheelchair/pushchair friendly, others involving stairs. Start at Boyes Beach, roughly 15 km from Rotorua (via SH30, Tarawera Rd, Okareka Loop Rd).

The loop features:
Village & Lakeside Views: From Boyes Beach to Millar Road DOC Campground, enjoy accessible paths through lakeside and residential areas.
Farmland & Native Bush: Head towards Acacia Road, traversing farmland and 3 km of regenerating native bush on the eastern side leading to the outlet.
Silver Beach & The Outlet: From Silver Beach (picnic/toilets), the track narrows, ascending with stairs through bush to the Lake Okareka outlet.
Wetland Boardwalk: Silver Beach to Acacia Rd offers a flat, accessible boardwalk through wetlands with a birdwatching hide.
Village Walk: Easy walkway return through a low density village.
Viewpoints offer sweeping lake views, often with Mount Tarawera.
